questions.question: What is the name of the operation that inserts a new node into a binary tree?
questions.explanation: Insertion is the operation that adds a new node to a binary tree.

questions.question: Which of the following binary tree operations has a time complexity of O(n)?
questions.explanation: Searching for a node in a binary tree can be done in O(n) time, where n is the number of nodes in the tree.

questions.question: What is the purpose of the inorder traversal of a binary tree?
options: To traverse the tree in ascending order
questions.explanation: Inorder traversal visits nodes in ascending order. It is commonly used for binary search trees.

questions.question: Which of the following binary tree operations is an example of a recursive operation?
questions.explanation: Node deletion in a binary tree is typically performed recursively to ensure that the tree remains balanced.

questions.question: What is the time complexity of traversing a binary tree using a depth-first search?
questions.explanation: The time complexity of a depth-first search on a binary tree is O(n), where n is the number of nodes in the tree.
